title: Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI) - Adapted
description: An adapted version of the PSQI to help you assess your overall sleep quality across seven key areas. This self-assessment provides insight into patterns that may be affecting your rest.
instructions: Think about your typical sleep patterns over the past month. Answer each question based on what has been most common for you.

questions.text: Waking up during the night or early morning
Having to get up to use the bathroom
Feeling too cold or too hot
Overall rating of your sleep quality
Needing medication or supplements to sleep
Trouble staying awake during daytime activities
Lack of enthusiasm or motivation to get things done

labels.3: Three or more times a week

bands.label: Good Sleep Quality
bands.description: Your responses suggest good overall sleep quality. Continue your current sleep habits and use this course to further optimize your rest.

bands.label: Mild Sleep Difficulties
bands.description: Your responses suggest some sleep difficulties. The techniques in this course can help you improve specific areas. Pay attention to which questions scored highest.

bands.label: Moderate Sleep Problems
bands.description: Your responses suggest moderate sleep problems that are likely affecting your daily life. The strategies in this course are designed for exactly this level of difficulty. Consider also consulting a healthcare provider.

bands.label: Significant Sleep Disturbance
bands.description: Your responses suggest significant sleep disturbance. We strongly recommend consulting a healthcare provider or sleep specialist alongside the educational content in this course.

scoring.functionalImpairmentQuestion: How much of a problem has poor sleep been for your overall quality of life during the past month?

disclaimer: This is an adapted educational self-check tool based on PSQI concepts, not a clinical diagnosis. Results are meant to help you reflect on your sleep patterns. If you are in crisis or need immediate help, please call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline).
